key_departures.impact: Bush was the Browns' lone defensive starter hitting free agency. He posted career highs with 125 tackles, 3 INTs, and 8 PDs in 2025. Cleveland chose not to match his $10M/yr asking price, prioritizing the offensive line overhaul.

Njoku announced he would not return to Cleveland in 2026. He was the team's starting TE and the centerpiece of the passing offense in prior years. His departure leaves Harold Fannin Jr. as the clear TE1 with thin depth behind him.
Teller announced his departure publicly. A long-time starting right guard for Cleveland, his exit is one of four Week 1 OL starters lost. Released with post-June 1 designation.

Seven-time Pro Bowler's contract voided, carrying a $23.5M dead-cap charge. Bitonio is contemplating retirement. His departure—by retirement or free agency—removes an elite anchor at left guard. The signing of Zion Johnson was directly driven by anticipation of his exit.
Conklin was released and is a free agent. He was a former All-Pro who struggled to stay healthy. The acquisition of Tytus Howard signals Cleveland has moved on from Conklin at right tackle.

Starting center is rehabbing a torn Achilles sustained in Week 14 of 2025. His contract voided, making him a free agent. Unlikely to return given injury timeline and the signing of Elgton Jenkins at C/G.
Left tackle starter departed as a free agent. One of four Week 1 OL starters lost. Left tackle remains the single most pressing remaining need on the offensive line after FA additions.
Rotational defensive end logged 310 snaps in 2025. Modest loss of depth. Not a starter-level departure and does not materially alter the EDGE need picture.

signings.analysis: Howard is a versatile veteran who has started at RT, RG, and LG. The 3yr/$63M extension with $43M guaranteed is a real commitment and he projects as the starting right tackle. However, left tackle remains completely unaddressed — Dawand Jones (recovering from knee surgery) is the only other tackle under contract. OT is partially addressed: the right side is now stable, but LT is a critical draft priority. The trade cost was minimal (one of three 5th-round picks Cleveland held).

Johnson (26) was arguably the top guard on the free agent market. He's a former No. 17 overall pick with 65 career starts, primarily at left guard. The 3yr/$32.4M guaranteed deal is a legitimate investment in a starting-caliber player in his prime. He ranked second in run block win rate in 2025 — ideal for Todd Monken's run-first identity. IOL is partially addressed: Johnson fills one guard spot, and Elgton Jenkins addresses center/the other guard, but the overall line still has open competition and left tackle void. Cleveland utilized five void years to keep the cap hit manageable (~$5M in 2026).

Two-time Pro Bowl lineman with experience at center, guard, and tackle. Released by Green Bay with a 'failed physical' designation after fracturing his fibula and suffering ligament damage in his ankle in November 2025, the same day he signed with Cleveland — indicating the Browns are betting on his recovery. The 2yr/$24M with $20M guaranteed is significant investment. He projects to start at center with Zion Johnson at left guard. Combined with Johnson, Teven Jenkins re-sign, and Howard, the interior line has been substantially rebuilt — but health risk is real and LT remains open.

Re-signed on an undisclosed one-year deal (prior deal was 1yr/$3M). A second-round pick (No. 39) by Chicago in 2021, Jenkins started four games at right guard after Teller was benched and appeared in all 17 games. He's a swing starter/depth piece but not a long-term solution. With Johnson, Elgton Jenkins, and Zak Zinter already on roster, Teven Jenkins is depth competition at RG. Low-cost, low-risk retention. Contract terms not yet publicly reported — valued here as a veteran minimum-equivalent.

Williams was signed within hours of Devin Bush departing for Chicago. He's a 2023 first-team All-Pro with ties to new DC Mike Rutenberg, who coached him in both Jacksonville and New York over five seasons. Williams made 100+ tackles in each of his four seasons under Rutenberg and brings speed and coverage ability that complement 2025 Defensive Rookie of the Year Carson Schwesinger. However, his 2025 was a down year (83 tackles, 45.5 PFF grade in 13 games), and the 2yr/$17M deal lacks the guarantees of a top-of-market LB signing. At best a lateral move from Bush in 2025 production — need partially addressed with clear scheme-fit upside via Rutenberg connection. Guarantee figure not yet publicly confirmed; structure noted as TBD.

Veteran minimum-level signing ($1.315M per OTC/Spotrac) to pair with Harold Fannin Jr. after Njoku's departure. Stoll is an inline blocking TE — 81% of career snaps from inline alignment. He has just 28 career receptions. He is not a receiving replacement for Njoku; rather, he is a complementary blocker to give Monken two-TE sets for the run game. The one-year, near-minimum deal signals he is a TE2/TE3, not a solution. TE remains a legitimate draft need for a receiving option to pair with Fannin.

Hickman was tendered as a restricted free agent at the second-round tender level ($5.767M). He started all 17 games at safety in 2025. This is a retention move that keeps an ascending young starter in the fold. S was not a pre-FA need and this tender confirms it is not a need going forward. Any team signing Hickman would owe Cleveland a 2nd-round pick, making him effectively locked in.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Howard (3yr/$63M, $43M gtd) firmly addresses right tackle. Tytus Howard is committed as a starter through 2028. However, left tackle is completely unaddressed — Cam Robinson and Jack Conklin are both gone, and Dawand Jones is recovering from knee surgery. LT is now a critical Day 1 or Day 2 draft target. OT drops from a blanket Tier 1 need to Tier 2 specifically because RT is now solved but LT is urgent. Expect the Browns to target a LT at pick No. 6 or 24.

The Browns lost all five OL starters and have substantially rebuilt the interior: Zion Johnson (3yr/$49.5M, starts at LG), Elgton Jenkins (2yr/$24M, starts at C — health caveat), Teven Jenkins (depth/swing RG), and Zak Zinter (2024 3rd-round pick). Bitonio's likely retirement was already priced in. The main IOL risk is Elgton Jenkins' ankle fracture recovery. If he's healthy, the interior is a genuine strength. Drops from Tier 1 to Tier 3 — residual depth and developmental IOL still useful in mid-rounds but no longer urgent.

Bush departure created a hole; Williams signing partially fills it. The Rutenberg connection is genuine and Williams has starter pedigree (2023 All-Pro). Pairing with Schwesinger gives Cleveland a plausible two-LB starting unit. However, Williams' decline in 2024-25 and age (29 turning 30) introduce uncertainty. LB drops one tier — still a mid-round draft target for depth and developmental competition, but no longer a top-3 priority.

Njoku's departure is a significant loss. Stoll's near-minimum signing does not address the receiving TE need — he is a blocker. Harold Fannin Jr. is a legitimate TE1 after leading the team in receiving as a rookie, but Cleveland needs a legitimate receiving TE2 to unlock two-TE sets in Monken's offense. TE need is unchanged — rounds 2-4 remain a realistic target window.

Zero WR signings. Cleveland's WR room produced just 1,467 receiving yards in 2025 — the worst in the NFL. No additions have been made. WR remains a critical need and should be addressed in rounds 1-3 of the draft or potentially via a later trade.

No QB additions made. Watson is still on the books with a massive restructured cap hit. Shedeur Sanders (rookie), Dillon Gabriel, and Joe Flacco rotated in 2025. The quarterback situation is entirely unresolved and is effectively the franchise's defining question heading into the draft. QB remains a Tier 1 need.

key_additions.headline: Versatile veteran RT acquired via trade + extension anchors a completely rebuilt offensive line
Top guard on FA market fills critical interior void with elite run-blocking profile
Two-time Pro Bowl interior lineman projects as starting center; health is the key question
Former All-Pro LB reunites with DC Rutenberg to replace departed Devin Bush
Re-signed swing guard provides interior depth and starter competition at RG
Blocking TE specialist added to pair with Fannin Jr. after Njoku's departure
All-17-game starter retained on second-round RFA tender to anchor secondary

key_losses.headline: Career-best season (125 tackles, 3 INTs) earned Bush a $30M deal Cleveland chose not to match
Longtime starting TE officially released, leaving Fannin Jr. as the clear TE1 with thin depth
Seven-time Pro Bowl left guard's contract voided; likely retiring after 12 seasons in Cleveland
Starting RG formally cut after public farewell; one of four Week 1 OL starters lost
Injury-plagued former All-Pro RT released; Howard acquisition signals his replacement
Starting LT departed as UFA, leaving left tackle the lone unsolved OL hole heading to draft
Starting center's contract voided while rehabbing Achilles; Elgton Jenkins his likely replacement
Rotational DE departs for Atlanta; modest depth loss that does not escalate EDGE need

notes: Teven Jenkins re-sign contract terms (length, value) have not been publicly reported as of March 13, 2026. Flagged as undisclosed; role assessed as depth/rotational.
Quincy Williams 2yr/$17M guarantee structure not yet confirmed by OTC/Spotrac; total value confirmed by multiple reporters.

Howard contract figure discrepancy: ESPN initially reported 2yr/$45M extension (new money), while NFL.com, CBS, and Cleveland.com reported 3yr/$63M including existing year. OTC reflects a $56M figure on older Texans deal. The 3yr/$63M with $43M guaranteed from click2houston/NFL.com sourcing used as primary figure — noting this reflects total contract commitment including existing year.

Joel Bitonio's status as of March 13 is still technically undecided (not signed elsewhere, not officially announced retirement), but his contract has voided and the Browns have signed replacements at his position.
Martin Emerson Jr. (CB, ACL recovery) listed as 'terms not yet known' for a re-sign in some trackers. Not included in signings until contract terms are confirmed.
Cap space of $8,649,433 per Over The Cap as of March 11, 2026 close of business.
